original_hypothesis.title: Governance address in Deploy script is hard-coded and marked TODO: misconfiguration can irreversibly assign admin powers incorrectly
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Deploy.sol hardcodes a governance address with a TODO comment. If deployment uses an incorrect or placeholder governance address, admin control of VotingEscrow/GaugeController/LendingLedger could be permanently assigned to the wrong entity, preventing correct setup, market onboarding, reward configuration, and emergency actions.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a realistic operational misconfiguration risk with high blast radius. While not an on-chain exploit by an unprivileged attacker, it commonly occurs in production deployments and is difficult to recover from without redeploying and migrating users.
deployment_assumptions: Production deployment uses Deploy.sol (or copied parameters) without correctly setting the governance address
No subsequent on-chain mechanism exists for the intended governance to claim control (only current governance can call setGovernance)
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: Deploy.sol sets `address governance = address(0x169F...); // TODO` and passes it into all constructors; no post-deploy ownership transfer calls are made.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Hard-coded governance address with TODO; constructors use this value; no post-deploy setGovernance calls

preconditions: Contracts are deployed using the provided script or equivalent params
Governance address is incorrect/uncontrolled by intended operators

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Admin key is configured only at deployment; wrong value leads to irreversible control loss

operational_questions: What is the intended governance address/multisig and how is it validated before deployment?
Is there a deployment checklist verifying ownership/governance addresses on-chain after deploy?
Are there contingency plans for redeploy/migration if governance is mis-set?
validation_requirements: Review actual deployment transactions and confirm governance addresses in constructors
Verify governance can call setRewards/whitelist/add_gauge on deployed instances
Check if any timelock/multisig wrapper is intended but missing

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Exploit deployment misconfiguration (wrong governance address) so intended governance lacks control
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: If attacker controls the configured address, they can execute privileged operations
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Protocol cannot be operated as intended; users must migrate

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: Potential full admin control if wrong governance is attacker-controlled
potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Protocol becomes unmanaged or maliciously managed; inability to onboard markets or set rewards correctly
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: High; may require redeploy and user migration
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: All onlyGovernance actions across core contracts

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Governance address equals placeholder; governance txs failing from intended operators
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Unexpected admin actions from unknown address

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
